summary.intent: The user was conducting an iterative multi-pass review cycle (pass 4) to fix bugs in Rust UI input components (PinInput, SearchInput, NumberInput, PasswordInput) identified through GitHub PR review threads.
summary.outcome: 8 review threads (2 P1, 5 P2, 1 P3) were addressed: NumberInput got a round_and_clamp helper fixing rounding-then-clamp ordering, SearchInput gained debounce_pending context tracking and Focus-during-loading state preservation, PinInput got RTL navigation support and Delete/Backspace distinction with a new ClearCell event, and PasswordInput gained a missing Change event. 1984 tests pass (+10 new), all clippy and xci-fast gates are green, and changes were committed and pushed.

repo: The project uses a multi-pass PR review workflow where an automated agent polls GitHub review threads in batches, with each pass building on fixes from prior passes.
Component state machines live in crates/ars-components/src/input/<component>/mod.rs and follow a consistent Event enum + transition arm + Api method pattern.
Property-based state machine tests are in crates/ars-components/tests/proptest_state_machines/input.rs and require explicit initialization of all context fields including new ones like `dir`.
The xci-fast gate is the pre-push quality gate (10 checks) that must pass before committing.
Spec documentation for components lives in spec/components/input/ and must be updated alongside code changes.
Clippy enforces missing_docs on public API items — new public enum variants and methods require doc comments.

code.finding: A round_and_clamp helper was introduced to fix a bug where rounding happened before clamping, causing values like 1.995 with max=1.99 to round to 2.0 before being clamped — the correct order is clamp first, then round. All round_to_precision call sites needed updating to use the new helper.

Added debounce_pending boolean to Context to fix RestartDebounce which previously had no way to know if a debounce was active. The has_debounce flag needed to be captured as a local bool before closures since props cannot be borrowed inside closures.

RTL navigation requires a dir field in both Props and Context. Delete key (clears current cell without moving focus) and Backspace key (moves to previous cell) must be handled as separate events — previously conflated. A new ClearCell event was added to support Delete semantics.
PasswordInput was entirely missing a Change event in its Event enum, transition arm, and Api method (on_input_change). This was a fundamental missing event that other input components already had.

workflow: Background thread polling for GitHub review notifications is unreliable in this agent setup — the workaround is falling back to direct gh api calls to check PR state.
Snapshot tests for aria attribute changes need explicit acceptance (cargo test -- --update-snapshots or equivalent) after intentional rendering changes.
When adding new fields to a Context/Props struct used in proptest state machine tests, the test initializers must also be updated or tests will fail to compile.

friction: Background completion notification polling kept dropping, requiring a fallback to direct gh api calls for each pass — this is a recurring reliability issue with the agent's background thread.
Closure capture rules in Rust required extracting props.debounce.is_some() into a local variable before the closure, causing a compile error mid-implementation that needed diagnosis.
Multiple Edit iterations were needed on number_input/mod.rs to find and update all round_to_precision call sites (required Bash grep + sequential reads between edits).

Adding dir to PinInput required touching Props struct, Context struct, init block, SetProps arm, props_output_changed function, on_cell_keydown function, and the proptest initializer — a high coordination cost for one new field.
open_items: Pass 5 was triggered at the end of this session — there are likely additional review threads from the round 4 changes that will need addressing.
The background thread polling bug in the agent infrastructure is a known recurring issue that has not been fixed, only worked around.
PinInput RTL support was added at the state machine level but the spec update should be verified to ensure it documents all RTL navigation behaviors correctly.
